experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally near the top, spanning columns 3-6 with an empty board. There are no immediate threats, so the goal is to place it efficiently to set up future pieces J, L, S, T. On an empty board, dropping the horizontal I flat is the safest and preserves a low, even surface. Moving or rotating first is unnecessary; hard dropping immediately reaches the bottom and advances toward building a clean stack.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: J piece is near the top in spawn orientation, and the board is almost empty except a horizontal I line resting on the bottom row center. Immediate threat is low; goal is to place pieces efficiently to build a flat stack and avoid creating holes. A good short-term sub-goal is to move the J toward an edge before dropping, since hard dropping now in the center would create awkward height over the existing I. Candidate actions: left/right start repositioning; rotations are not yet necessary on an open board; soft/hard drop commit too early. Moving left begins guiding the J toward the left side for a cleaner placement and leaves flexibility for later rotations.

experiences.intentions: The board is almost empty except for a settled I-piece lying horizontally on the bottom row from columns 3-6, and the active J is near the top in its spawn orientation. With such a clean field, the main goal is to place pieces efficiently to keep the stack flat and set up future placements from the upcoming L,S,T,O. Dropping the current J immediately in its present orientation is strong: it can land on the floor with its 3-wide base and one block above the left side, creating only a shallow 1-cell step rather than a deep hole. Rotating now is unnecessary, and lateral movement is not yet clearly better than simply locking in a low, safe placement. Hard drop advances that goal fastest.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high at the spawn area with an uneven but low stack below. My goal now is to place the T efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally keeping the stack flat and preserving options for upcoming O, Z, T, L pieces. experiences.intentions: Active L is high near the top with an empty field below until the existing stack starts in the lower half. Immediate threat is low, so the best sub-goal is efficient placement planning rather than emergency descent. The lower stack has a left-leaning contour and upcoming Z,I,S,O favor keeping the surface flatter. From the spawn position, moving the L toward the left wall is a strong setup because L can later drop into the left-side terrain more cleanly than staying central-right, and there is ample time before locking. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit far too early without alignment; soft_drop reduces planning time; rotations are not yet necessary because movement space is open; right moves away from the more useful left-side placement. Therefore the best current action is to shift left.
